title: Are Dermal Fillers Vegan？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Are Dermal Fillers Vegan？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: Are fillers vegan none of the fillers I'm aware of these days contain animal products back in the day when we were using collagen they tended to be made out of animal products so there was poor sign and bovine collagen which basically means pig and cow collagen and obviously we d…

segments.text: are fillers vegan none of the fillers I'm aware of these days contain animal products back in the day when we were using collagen they tended to be made out of animal products so there was poor sign and bovine collagen which basically means pig and cow collagen and obviously we don't use those anymore they weren't very good very common to be allergic to them didn't last that long so we now use synthetic regenerated collagen there's or they're actually often made from bacteria that I don't used where the gene has been changed and they produce lots and lots of hyaluronic acid as a result so we've basically farm bacteria that is that it's harmless to humans and that's where it comes from if you if you're worried about animal testing that's a different answer because nearly all the products have been tested it's somewhere along the line on an animal model and that's the only way that we can get them safe in a biological structure before we treat them on people use them on people they tend to be done these days in as a humane way as

possible which usually means one injection and then the rat is left to live its life and they just monitor it every six months to see what the bump has done they actually usually put the bump on the back of their heads just a small amount and then monitor it at least that is what I learned when I went to a dermal filler Factory now Botox is very different Botox unfortunately some of the manufacturers validate every single batch by looking for the ld50 that's the lethal dose double kill and a genetically homogeneous population of mice it kills 50% of the mice and that's how they tell our strong a batch is which is very sad for those mice and you can now choose dermal filler / botox products that are only validated using cellular assays so alig and do that the original brand of Botox is no longer validated on animals although of course that was also basically investigated originally our using animal models and many animals have been used to find out how that product works but if you're concerned about animal welfare and none of them are completely squeaky clean as are most
of the products that we use unfortunately but they are making great efforts to do and less animal testing [Music]
